title: Kernel bootstrap lane subagent7. Inspect only repo tests/smoke checks around slash commands and launcher installation, such as tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh, tests/test-k-remote.sh, and related scripts. Do not edit. Return the exact commands that should pass after a fix and any likely gaps betwe...
summary_text: For the slash-command + launcher surface you asked about, the post-fix command set to validate should be: - `bash tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` - `RUN_CODEX_KERNEL_SMOKE=1 bash tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` - `bash scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` - `bash tests/test-kernel-launcher.sh` - `bash tests/test-kernel-launcher-template.sh` - `bash tests/test-install-kernel-launchers.sh` - `bash tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh` - `bash tests/test-kernel-entrypoint.sh` - `bash tests/test-k-remote.sh` (manual/adjacent; not listed in CI) CI-related exact checks around this area are currently: - `bash -n tests/test-kernel-launcher.sh` - `bash -n tests/test-kernel-launcher-template.sh` - `bash -n tests/test-install-kernel-launchers.sh` - `bash -n tests/test-kernel-entrypoint.sh` - `bash -n tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh` - then in the “Kernel local adapter tests” job: - `bash tests/test-kernel-launcher.sh` - `bash tests/test-kernel-launcher-template.sh` - `bash tests/test-kernel-entrypoint.sh` - `bash tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh` - `bash tests/test-install-kernel-launchers.sh` - `bash tests/test-vote-launchers.sh` - `bash tests/test-codex-skill-launch.sh` Likely gaps versus real “slash command cannot be used” symptom: - `bash tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` mostly validates prompt/docs/static content, not actual Codex UI slash parsing or whether `/kernel`/`/k` are callable in the running Codex client. - `test-kernel-launcher.sh` and `test-kernel-launcher-template.sh` exercise launcher binary behavior with stubs; they do not validate your real `HOME/bin/codex-prompt-launch`, `kernel-root`, `orchestrator-entrypoint-hint`, `PATH`, and login shell state in user terminals. - `scripts/local/codex-orchestrator.zsh` only routes when that snippet is actually sourced by `~/.zshrc` and actually active; tests confirm file presence/contents, not that current shell sessions are using it. - `test-k-remote.sh` exists and passes with stubs for SSH/kernel transport, but it is not enforced in CI, so regressions there can slip through. - installer checks ensure artifacts exist and match, but they do not guarantee that the installed prompts/launchers are loaded by Codex at runtime. - README explicitly says bare `/kernel` is upstream-dependent, so failures can come from upstream Codex UI behavior even when launcher tests pass.
